question: I canceled my subscription last night but got an email this morning saying it will renew tomorrow. I’m worried I’ll be charged—can you help me make sure auto-renew is actually off?
reference_answer: I’m sorry for the stress—those reminder emails can lag behind the actual account status. Let’s verify auto-renew is truly disabled. Quick check: 1) Go to Account → Subscriptions 2) Open your plan and look for Renewal Status • You want to see “Auto-renew: Off” (or “Scheduled to end”) and a specific end date If it still shows “Renews on [date]”, toggle off auto-renew again and make sure you hit Confirm at the final step. Timing note: changes can take up to 15–30 minutes to reflect. If it’s typically typically within 24 hours of renewal, I recommend rechecking today and again a few hours before the renewal time. If it still won’t stick, tell me whether you subscribed on our website vs. iOS/Android, and the plan name. I’ll guide the right route and, if needed, flag it for escalation so you don’t have to keep chasing it.

question: I’m trying to stop my subscription from renewing next month, but I don’t see an “auto-renew” toggle anywhere. Where is it and will I lose access immediately?
reference_answer: Sorry for the runaround—let’s get that renewal turned off. In most accounts, the switch is under: Account → Subscriptions (or Membership) → Your Plan → Manage → Auto-Renew. Toggle Auto-Renew off, then confirm. Turning off auto-renew typically stops the next charge but keeps your access active until the end of your current billing period (you’ll see an “Active until” date on that same page). You should also receive a confirmation email within a few minutes. If you don’t see the toggle, you may be subscribed through Apple/Google or PayPal. Check the “Billed via” line on the plan page—if it says a third party, you’ll need to cancel in that provider’s subscription settings. If you tell me what device you’re on (web/iOS/Android), I can point you to the exact menu.

question: I tried cancelling auto-renew in the app but it keeps flipping back on. I’m worried I’ll get charged again. What should I do?
reference_answer: Sorry about the runaround — if the toggle keeps switching back, it’s usually a sync issue or the subscription is managed through a different channel. Quick checks: 1) In the app, go to Account → Subscriptions → Your Plan and confirm whether it says “Billed by us” or “Billed via Apple/Google.” 2) If it’s Apple/Google: you’ll need to disable renewal in your device settings (Apple: Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions; Google: Play Store → Payments & subscriptions → Subscriptions). 3) If it’s billed by us: try updating once on the website (not the app): Account → Subscriptions → Manage → Turn off auto-renew. After you switch it off, you should as the next step see a confirmation and an end-of-term date. If your renewal is in the next 48 hours, share the plan name and renewal date shown on that screen and I’ll help verify it’s safely set to not renew.

question: I received the wrong SKU in my delivery today. The packing slip shows order <ORDER_ID> (2x “AeroWeave Crew, Black, M”), but the box has “AeroWeave V-Neck, Navy, L”. Tracking says Delivered 11:08 AM and there’s a photo of the package at my door. Here’s the quickest path: 1) Upload 2 photos: (a) the item tag/label showing size/color and (b) the packing slip (or the box label if no slip). A single photo that captures both is fine. 2) Reply with your preferred delivery address confirmation (no need to retype it—just “address on order is correct” or “needs update”). Once I have those, I’ll open a “wrong item received” case and release an overnight reship if inventory allows. Cutoff is typically 2 PM local warehouse time; otherwise it ships next business day. You won’t need to return the incorrect item until the replacement is in motion—we’ll email a prepaid label. If overnight isn’t available, I’ll offer the fastest alternative (2-day) or a refund.

question: Shipment marked DELIVERED but the box contains a different product line entirely. Tracking shows a sort-center rewrap scan yesterday. I’m traveling in 2 days—can you reroute a replacement to a different address, and do you require a police report or just photos?

The “rewrap” scan is a strong indicator the carrier repackaged the parcel, which can lead to content swaps—so photos are typically enough. To get a replacement out quickly, please send: (1) photos of the outer box showing the shipping label and any rewrap tape/sticker, (2) photos of everything inside (including inserts), and (3) a close-up of the wrong item’s barcode/SKU. If you can, include the delivery confirmation page screenshot showing DELIVERED. Support can ship the replacement to a new address if it’s within the same country/region as the original order. Share the order <ORDER_ID> and the preferred shipping address via the secure address form (Account → Orders → order <ORDER_ID> → Update delivery options). Once verified, we’ll dispatch typically typically within 24 hours; if it can’t arrive before your trip, I can also offer a refund or hold-for-pickup option. No police report needed unless the carrier explicitly requests it later.

question: I received the wrong SKU in my order (got the 128GB version instead of 256GB). The outer label shows the correct item, so this looks like a pick/pack error. I need a replacement shipped today—can you do an advance replacement if I upload photos? Also, I don’t want to be charged twice.

I’ll flag this for a priority replacement as soon as we verify with photos. Once I have those, I can approve an advance replacement (we ship the correct 256GB today if it’s before our 2pm warehouse cutoff; otherwise next business day). On billing: we place a temporary authorization only if advance replacement requires it, and it drops off automatically within 3–5 business days after the return scan. If you prefer no authorization, we can do a standard exchange: we ship after the carrier shows the return in transit. Share your order <ORDER_ID> and confirm the delivery address city/state (no full address needed here).

question: order <ORDER_ID> delivered yesterday, but the box contains a completely different item (kitchen utensil set) and my item was a limited-run hoodie that’s now out of stock. I can provide photos and weight info from the carrier. What are my options, and can you do a replacement from a different warehouse or force-ship from returns inventory?

First, please send photos of: the item you received, the packing slip (if included), and the shipping label (you can redact your street address). If you have the carrier’s delivered weight from tracking, include that too—it helps us confirm the pack-out discrepancy. Next steps Support will take: (1) pull the pick/pack scan history (bin location, pack station, and weight check), (2) check cross-warehouse availability and any reserved units, and (3) query returns/QA inventory—if we have verified, unopened units, we can sometimes allocate one. If we can’t source a replacement within 24–48 hours, you can choose: a full refund to the original payment method (typically 3–5 business days after processing) or store credit plus a goodwill credit. If you don’t see an update from me typically typically within 2 business days, Support will escalate to our fulfillment lead. Please provide the order <ORDER_ID> and the hoodie size/color so I can search inventory accurately.

question: My delivery shows 'Delivered, front desk' but I got someone else’s item (different name on the packing slip). I manage a small office mailroom and can provide timestamps/camera stills. How do you handle chain-of-custody and avoid me being charged for a second shipment?
reference_answer: That’s a valid concern—if you received a package addressed to someone else, we’ll treat it as a mis-fulfillment and you shouldn’t be on the hook for replacement costs. Let me investigate the shipment events first (delivery scan type, geo-tag if available, and package weight vs. expected). To speed verification, please send: a photo of the packing slip showing the other recipient name/order (you can redact address), a photo of the shipping label on the box, and one photo of the item received. If you have a camera still, that’s helpful but not required. Next steps: I’ll file a carrier claim + internal pick/pack review and as the next step queue a replacement for your correct item (priority shipping where available). You’ll get a prepaid label to return the misdelivered item; if it’s not yours, we can also arrange a carrier pickup. If replacement isn’t confirmed typically typically within 24 hours, I’ll escalate to our fulfillment lead and update you—no need to chase us. Please provide your order <ORDER_ID> and confirm the correct delivery address (city/state only is fine here).

question: We’re on iOS and the subscription was originally started via Apple. I’ll help you disable it the correct way and verify what to expect. To turn off auto-renew (even if the app is deleted): 1) On your iPhone/iPad: Settings  your name (Apple ID)  Subscriptions. 2) Select our subscription  toggle off Auto-Renew or tap “Cancel Subscription.” 3) Confirm the “Expires” date shown—your access typically continues until that date. To confirm on our side: log in on the web  Account  Billing  Subscription. It should continue to show “Managed by App Store,” and typically typically within 24 hours Apple’s status usually syncs to “Expires on [date].” If it still shows renewing after 24 hours, share the subscription tier and the expiry date (no Apple receipt needed), and I’ll flag it for a manual sync check.

question: I’m trying to disable auto-renew for my Pro plan, but I subscribed through Apple and your site only shows “Managed by iTunes.” I also have two active subscriptions showing up (monthly + annual) after upgrading. Since your plan shows “Managed by iTunes,” we can’t directly turn off auto-renew on our website; Apple controls renewal and billing. Here’s the safest path: 1) On your iPhone/iPad: Settings → [your name] → Subscriptions → select the app/service → Cancel Subscription (this stops future renewal; access typically remains until the current paid period ends). 2) If you see both monthly and annual: open each entry and confirm which one has the next billing date. Cancel the one you don’t want first. 3) After canceling, wait ~5–15 minutes, then reopen the app and go to Account → Subscription to confirm status updates. If both are still active after 24 hours, tell me the renewal dates shown (no screenshots needed) and I’ll flag it for a billing review and help you request a duplicate-charge resolution with Apple.

question: I need to disable auto-renew immediately, but the toggle is greyed out because my plan was purchased through Apple. I can’t risk another renewal charge next week. What exactly do I do, and how do I confirm it’s actually off?
reference_answer: Totally understand—App Store-managed subscriptions can be stressful because the renewal control lives with Apple, not us. Let me help you shut it off and verify it. On your iPhone/iPad: Settings → [your name] → Subscriptions → select our subscription → tap “Cancel Subscription” (or “Cancel Free Trial”). If you don’t see it there, check the Apple ID you used at purchase. To confirm it’s disabled: the subscription should show an “Expires” date (not “Renews”) and no upcoming billing date. Apple typically keeps access active until the end of the current period. If you were charged after canceling, Apple’s billing timestamp can lag a few hours—wait 2–4 hours, then re-check. If it still shows renewing, tell me the plan name and renewal date shown, and I’ll flag your account on our side to prevent duplicate service activation.

question: I manage billing for a small team account. We’re on annual, paid via Apple IAP on iOS, but the web dashboard shows “managed externally” and won’t let me disable auto-renew. We need to prevent renewal while keeping seats active until the anniversary date. What’s the correct sequence, and how do we confirm it’s actually off?
reference_answer: You’re seeing the right thing—when a subscription is billed through Apple, we can’t flip auto-renew from our web dashboard. The change has to be made in Apple’s billing, and your access will remain active through the already-paid annual term. Do this from the Apple ID that owns the subscription: iPhone/iPad Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions → select our app/subscription → Cancel Subscription (or turn off Auto-Renew, depending on iOS version). Apple will show an “Expires on” date—take note of that. Confirmation steps: within 1–2 hours (sometimes up to 24), our dashboard should update to “Expires on [date]” / “Renews: Off (Apple).” No action needed on our side. If it still shows renewing after 24 hours, share a screenshot of the subscription page status (no receipt numbers) and the renewal date shown in Apple, and I’ll flag it to our billing team to sync the entitlement.

question: I’m on an annual plan billed through Apple, but your web dashboard still shows “Renews on Apr 14” and I can’t find an auto-renew toggle. I need to stop renewal without canceling access early. What’s the exact flow, and how do I confirm it’s actually off?
reference_answer: Totally understand—Apple-managed subscriptions don’t expose an auto-renew switch on our site, so the dashboard can look confusing. Let me walk you through the clean way to disable renewal while keeping access until the end of the paid term. 1) On iPhone/iPad: Settings → Apple ID (your name) → Subscriptions → select our app/service → toggle off “Auto-Renew” (or tap “Cancel Subscription,” which in Apple’s wording stops future renewal but keeps access until Apr 14). 2) On Mac: App Store → your name → Account Settings → Subscriptions → Manage → select us → Cancel. Confirmation: Apple will show an “Expires” date instead of “Renews,” usually within a few minutes (sometimes up to 24 hours). Our dashboard may continue to show “Renews” until we receive Apple’s status sync. If it still shows renewing after 24 hours, send a screenshot of the Subscriptions page (no personal details) and the plan name, and I’ll flag a refresh on our side.

question: I’m trying to disable auto-renew on my annual plan, but the toggle is greyed out and it says “Managed by Apple.” I don’t want another charge next month. What’s the exact path to stop renewal, and how do I confirm it actually worked?
reference_answer: Totally understand—when a subscription shows “Managed by Apple,” we can’t switch auto-renew off from our side, but you can stop the next renewal directly in iOS. Here’s the clean path: 1) On your iPhone/iPad: Settings → your Apple ID (top) → Subscriptions. 2) Select our subscription → tap “Cancel Subscription” (this disables auto-renew; it doesn’t as the next step remove access). 3) Confirm cancellation. To verify it worked: in the same Subscriptions screen, it should show an “Expires” date (not “Renews”) and no upcoming billing amount. Timing note: if you cancel at least 24 hours before the renewal time, you shouldn’t be charged again. If you still see a pending renewal after canceling, tell me the platform (iOS version) and the expiration date shown—no need to share your Apple ID—and I’ll help you sanity-check it.

question: I’m on the annual Pro plan and I need auto-renew OFF, but I can’t risk losing access before my term ends. If I cancel today, do you terminate immediately or just stop the next renewal? Also, I don’t see the toggle in the web app—only on mobile.
reference_answer: Totally understand—turning off auto-renew shouldn’t put your current paid term at risk. In our system, disabling auto-renew stops the next renewal charge; it does not end your access early. Your plan stays active through the “Paid through” date shown on your account. Here’s the quickest way to do it on web: 1) Account (top-right) → Billing → Subscription 2) Under “Renewal,” select “Turn off auto-renew” (or “Cancel renewal”) 3) Confirm, then verify the status changes to “Renews: Off” and note the end date If you subscribed via Apple/Google, the toggle won’t appear on web—manage it in the app store instead: iOS Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions, or Google Play → Payments & subscriptions → Subscriptions. If you tell me whether you subscribed on web, iOS, or Android (no card details), I’ll point you to the exact path for your setup.

question: I received a completely different SKU than what my invoice shows. The packing slip says 128GB, but the device in the box is 64GB (I checked in settings). question: The box shows my order <ORDER_ID>, but the item inside is someone else’s color/size and the seal was intact. question: I received the wrong color/variant and the serial number doesn’t match the invoice. I’m worried this will complicate warranty registration later. question: I received the wrong SKU in my delivery—box label shows my order, but inside is a different model/color. I need a replacement shipped today because this is for an event in 3 days. Also, I don’t want to be charged again or wait for the return to be processed first. What’s the fastest path?

question: I received the wrong SKU in my shipment—ordered the 2-pack 60W USB-C chargers (black), got a single 30W unit (white). I’m traveling tomorrow morning, so I need a replacement shipped today. question: I received the wrong SKU—box says “Pro 2-Pack, Black,” but inside are the white singles. I’m traveling Tuesday morning and need the correct item delivered to my hotel by Monday. question: I initiated a return and the portal says “label created,” but I never received an email and I need a QR code drop-off option (no printer). Also, the item was opened for inspection—does that void the return?

If you can share the return ID (preferred) or order <ORDER_ID>, Support can trigger a new delivery to your email on file—no need to post it here if you’d rather use the in-app chat. For printer-free returns: once re-sent, open the label link and select “QR code” (or “Show code at drop-off”)—the carrier will print the label at the counter. On condition: opening the box to inspect is fine. Returns are typically rejected only if the product is used beyond inspection, missing accessories/manuals, or has damage not from shipping. If you’re unsure, send a quick photo of what you’re returning and the packaging contents, and I’ll confirm eligibility before you ship. If the QR doesn’t arrive within 15 minutes, I’ll generate an alternate label as the next step.

question: We’re returning 3 units from a bulk order. The RMA generated one label only, but we need to split into two boxes due to carrier size limits. Can we use the same label twice? Also, one unit has a broken seal but is unused—will it be refused?
